title: LMEB: Long-horizon Memory Embedding Benchmark
task: Evaluating embedding models on long-horizon memory retrieval tasks
key_results: LMEB spans 22 datasets, 193 zero-shot tasks across 4 memory types; larger models don't always perform better; LMEB and MTEB are orthogonal
comments: Addresses a genuine gap in embedding evaluation by testing context-dependent, temporally distant information retrieval rather than static passage matching.

model_io.model: 15 embedding models (hundreds of millions to 10B parameters)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Larger embedding models perform better on long-horizon memory retrieval
hypotheses.result: False—model size does not guarantee better performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Performance on traditional passage retrieval (MTEB) transfers to memory retrieval
hypotheses.result: False—LMEB and MTEB exhibit orthogonality

title: Cheers: Decoupling Patch Details from Semantic Representations Enables Unified Multimodal Comprehension and Generation
task: Unified multimodal vision-language comprehension and generation
key_results: Matches/surpasses advanced UMMs on benchmarks; 4x token compression; outperforms Qwen1.5B on GenEval and MMBench with 20% training cost
comments: Novel decoupling of semantic tokens from patch details via gated residuals is elegant; the dual diffusion-autoregressive decoding is a key architectural contribution.

model_io.model: Cheers (unified vision tokenizer + LLM transformer + cascaded flow matching head)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Separating patch details from semantic representations improves both comprehension and generation stability
hypotheses.result: True—gated detail residuals enable efficient, high-quality multimodal generation

title: Can Vision-Language Models Solve the Shell Game?
task: Visual entity tracking in video for VLMs via spatiotemporal reasoning
key_results: Current VLMs perform at chance level on VET-Bench shell-game task; SGCoT + Molmo2 achieves >90% accuracy
comments: VET-Bench's synthetic design elegantly isolates the tracking deficiency by removing visual shortcuts; the theoretical analysis connecting to state-tracking and transformer expressivity is insightful.

model_io.model: Vision-Language Models (state-of-the-art VLMs) baseline; Molmo2 + SGCoT method

hypotheses.hypothesis: VLMs can track indistinguishable objects using static frame-level features
hypotheses.result: False—fixed-depth transformers fail without spatiotemporal continuity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit trajectory generation as intermediate states enables reliable tracking
hypotheses.result: True—SGCoT achieves >90% accuracy

title: OmniForcing: Unleashing Real-time Joint Audio-Visual Generation
task: Real-time streaming joint audio-visual generation via causal diffusion distillation
key_results: Achieves ~25 FPS streaming on single GPU; maintains multi-modal synchronization and visual quality of bidirectional teacher
comments: Addresses severe training instability from temporal asymmetry between modalities through clever mechanisms (Asymmetric Block-Causal Alignment, Audio Sink Token); represents significant practical contribution to streaming generation.

model_io.model: OmniForcing (distilled streaming autoregressive from bidirectional diffusion model)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Naive causal distillation of dual-stream bidirectional models is stable
hypotheses.result: False—extreme temporal asymmetry causes severe training instability requiring specialized alignment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Streaming audio-visual generation can maintain synchronization and quality at real-time rates
hypotheses.result: True—~25 FPS with proposed mechanisms

title: Video Streaming Thinking: VideoLLMs Can Watch and Think Simultaneously
task: Online streaming video understanding with concurrent reasoning during playback
key_results: VST-7B: 79.5% on StreamingBench, 59.3% on OVO-Bench; 15.7x faster than Video-R1 with +5.4% improvement
comments: The 'thinking while watching' paradigm elegantly amortizes LLM latency over video playback; knowledge-graph-based QA synthesis is a practical contribution.

model_io.model: VideoLLM with streaming causal architecture (VST-SFT + VST-RL)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Concurrent reasoning during video streaming improves responsiveness without sacrificing accuracy
hypotheses.result: True—15.7x speedup with +5.4% improvement over test-time scaling

title: Multimodal OCR: Parse Anything from Documents
task: Document parsing combining text and graphics into unified structured representations
key_results: 83.9% on olmOCR Bench; second only to Gemini 3 Pro on OCR Arena Elo; surpasses Gemini 3 Pro on SVG reconstruction (charts, UI, diagrams)
comments: Treating graphics as first-class parsing targets (not discarded pixels) is a paradigm shift; 3B compact model achieves competitive results at fraction of cost.

model_io.model: dots.mocr (3B parameter model with unified vision tokenizer and code generation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Jointly parsing text and graphics enables more faithful document reconstruction than separate pipelines
hypotheses.result: True—second-place on OCR Arena; state-of-the-art on graphics-to-code

title: daVinci-Env: Open SWE Environment Synthesis at Scale
task: Large-scale executable SWE agent training environments with quality-centric filtering
key_results: OpenSWE: 45,320 executable Docker environments across 12.8k repositories; OpenSWE-32B/72B achieve 62.4%/66.0% on SWE-bench Verified; +12 points on math, +5 on science (no factual recall degradation)
comments: Massive infrastructure investment ($1.47M) yields reproducible, open-source asset; difficulty-aware curation is key to learning efficiency.

model_io.model: SWE agents (Qwen2.5-32B/72B) trained on OpenSWE trajectories

hypotheses.hypothesis: SWE-focused training transfers to out-of-domain reasoning tasks without factual degradation
hypotheses.result: True—+12 math, +5 science, no recall loss

title: Visual-ERM: Reward Modeling for Visual Equivalence
task: Fine-grained visual reward modeling for vision-to-code RL with interpretable feedback
key_results: Visual-ERM improves Qwen3-VL-8B by +8.4 on chart-to-code, +2.7 table, +4.1 SVG; outperforms Qwen3-VL-235B on VC-RewardBench
comments: Rendering visual comparisons into reward signal is intuitive and avoids reward hacking; multimodal generative reward model is novel approach.

model_io.model: Visual-ERM (multimodal generative reward model)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-grained visual reward supervision is necessary for vision-to-code RL quality
hypotheses.result: True—consistent gains across tasks; visual embeddings insufficient

title: MM-CondChain: A Programmatically Verified Benchmark for Visually Grounded Deep Compositional Reasoning
task: Evaluating MLLMs on deep compositional reasoning with visually grounded conditions
key_results: MM-CondChain: strongest MLLM achieves only 53.33% Path F1; sharp drops on hard negatives, with depth/complexity increase
comments: Programmatically verified benchmark (VPIR) is elegant; reveals fundamental gap in compositional reasoning—not adequately tested by existing benchmarks.

model_io.model: Multimodal LLMs (various architectures)

hypotheses.hypothesis: MLLMs can perform deep compositional reasoning on visually grounded conditions
hypotheses.result: False—53.33% Path F1 indicates major capability gap

title: V-Bridge: Bridging Video Generative Priors to Versatile Few-shot Image Restoration
task: Few-shot image restoration via video generative model priors
key_results: Competitive restoration with <2% of training samples (1,000 samples) vs. traditional methods; single model handles multiple tasks rivaling specialized architectures
comments: Reframing restoration as progressive generation is conceptually elegant; demonstrates underutilized capacity in pre-trained video models.

model_io.model: Video generative models (pre-trained, fine-tuned for restoration)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video generative models contain transferable restoration priors activable with minimal data
hypotheses.result: True—<2% training data achieves competitive performance

title: EvoScientist: Towards Multi-Agent Evolving AI Scientists for End-to-End Scientific Discovery
task: Multi-agent evolving AI scientist framework with persistent memory for end-to-end discovery
key_results: Outperforms 7 SOTA systems in scientific idea generation (novelty, feasibility, relevance, clarity); improved code execution success via multi-agent evolution
comments: Persistent memory modules for ideas and experiments enable continuous improvement; distillation of insights from interaction history is pragmatic.

model_io.model: EvoScientist (Researcher Agent, Engineer Agent, Evolution Manager Agent with persistent memory)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Static pipelines outperform evolving multi-agent systems in scientific discovery
hypotheses.result: False—EvoScientist with persistent memory exceeds SOTA

title: From Sparse to Dense: Multi-View GRPO for Flow Models via Augmented Condition Space
task: Flow model alignment via multi-view reward mapping in dense condition space
key_results: MV-GRPO achieves superior alignment over SOTA; richer optimization signals from semantically adjacent captions
comments: Augmenting condition space for dense reward mapping is clever; avoids sample regeneration via probability distribution derivation.

model_io.model: Text-to-Image flow models (with GRPO fine-tuning)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-view evaluation with augmented conditions improves alignment vs. single-condition evaluation
hypotheses.result: True—denser reward mapping enables superior optimization

title: HomeSafe-Bench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Unsafe Action Detection for Embodied Agents in Household Scenarios
task: Unsafe action detection for embodied household agents via VLM streaming evaluation
key_results: HD-Guard balances latency and accuracy via hierarchical dual-brain (FastBrain + asynchronous SlowBrain); identifies critical VLM bottlenecks
comments: HomeSafe-Bench's hybrid simulation + video generation pipeline is practical; real-time safety monitoring requires decomposition into lightweight + deep reasoning.

model_io.model: HD-Guard (hierarchical streaming VLM architecture)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single-pass VLM evaluation suffices for real-time household safety monitoring
hypotheses.result: False—requires hierarchical lightweight + deep reasoning decomposition

title: HybridStitch: Pixel and Timestep Level Model Stitching for Diffusion Acceleration
task: Text-to-Image diffusion acceleration via hybrid model stitching at pixel and timestep levels
key_results: HybridStitch: 1.83x speedup on Stable Diffusion 3 vs. mixture-of-models baselines
comments: Treating generation as editing and separating easy vs. complex regions is intuitive; per-timestep region adaptation is more fine-grained than prior work.

model_io.model: HybridStitch (large + small diffusion models with region/timestep switching)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Uniform model replacement across timesteps is optimal for acceleration
hypotheses.result: False—region-adaptive switching achieves better speedup

title: VQQA: An Agentic Approach for Video Evaluation and Quality Improvement
task: Multi-agent video evaluation and iterative quality improvement via VLM critiques
key_results: VQQA: +11.57% T2V-CompBench, +8.43% VBench2 over vanilla; generalizes T2V and I2V; few refinement steps suffice
comments: Replacing passive metrics with actionable VLM critiques is pragmatic; closed-loop black-box prompt optimization avoids white-box access requirement.

model_io.model: VQQA (multi-agent with VLM critic and prompt optimizer)

hypotheses.hypothesis: VLM critiques enable effective video quality improvement without model access
hypotheses.result: True—substantial gains via black-box refinement

title: Think While Watching: Online Streaming Segment-Level Memory for Multi-Turn Video Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models
task: Online streaming multi-turn video reasoning with segment-level memory preservation
key_results: Improves StreamingBench by 2.6%, OVO-Bench by 3.79%; reduces multi-round output tokens by 56% while maintaining performance
comments: Segment-level causal masking + streaming positional encoding enables continuous memory in multi-turn interaction; overlapped watching-thinking pipeline is practical.

model_io.model: Think While Watching (streaming VideoLLM with segment-level memory)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Preserving segment-level memory across multi-turn interaction improves performance
hypotheses.result: True—+2.6% to +3.79% with token efficiency

title: Spend Less, Reason Better: Budget-Aware Value Tree Search for LLM Agents
task: Budget-aware multi-hop reasoning via value tree search with step-level intervention
key_results: BAVT: under strict low-budget constraints, surpasses baseline at 4x resource allocation; consistent wins on multi-hop QA
comments: Budget-conditioned node selection (remaining ratio as exponent) is principled; residual value predictor addresses overconfidence. Theoretical convergence guarantee is notable.

model_io.model: Budget-Aware Value Tree (single LLM backbone with value estimation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Brute-force compute scaling outperforms intelligent budget management in multi-hop reasoning
hypotheses.result: False—BAVT achieves SOTA with 4x fewer resources

title: LookaheadKV: Fast and Accurate KV Cache Eviction by Glimpsing into the Future without Generation
task: Fast KV cache eviction for long-context LLMs via lightweight future-gazing without draft generation
key_results: LookaheadKV: outperforms baselines on long-context benchmarks; reduces eviction cost by 14.5x vs. draft-based methods; negligible runtime overhead
comments: Parameter-efficient prediction of importance scores without explicit draft generation is elegant; 14.5x reduction in overhead vs. draft methods is significant.

model_io.model: LookaheadKV (transformer layers augmented with importance predictors)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Draft-based importance estimation is necessary for accurate KV eviction in long-context tasks
hypotheses.result: False—parameter-efficient prediction suffices with minimal overhead

title: Taking Shortcuts for Categorical VQA Using Super Neurons
task: Early-exit VQA for categorical tasks via super-neuron probing on raw activations
key_results: 5.10x speedup via extreme early exiting from first LLM layer at first token; improves classification while reducing latency
comments: Shifting from attention vectors to scalar neuron activations dramatically expands search space; early exiting from first layer is aggressive but feasible.

model_io.model: VLM with super-neuron probing classifiers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-grained attention head selection is necessary for VQA efficiency gains
hypotheses.result: False—raw neuron activations enable even more aggressive early exiting

title: Steve-Evolving: Open-World Embodied Self-Evolution via Fine-Grained Diagnosis and Dual-Track Knowledge Distillation
task: Open-world embodied agent self-evolution via fine-grained diagnosis and dual-track knowledge distillation
key_results: Steve-Evolving: consistent improvements over static-retrieval baselines in long-horizon Minecraft MCU tasks
comments: Three-tier experience indexing (condition signatures, spatial hashing, semantic tags) + compositional diagnosis signals enable auditable evolution. Non-parametric approach avoids retraining.

model_io.model: Steve-Evolving (experience anchoring, distillation, knowledge-driven control loop)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Persistent memory enables embodied agents to avoid repeating failed experiments
hypotheses.result: True—structured experience indexing + diagnosis-triggered replanning improves performance

title: NanoVDR: Distilling a 2B Vision-Language Retriever into a 70M Text-Only Encoder for Visual Document Retrieval
task: Visual document retrieval via asymmetric encoder distillation (2B teacher to 69M student)
key_results: NanoVDR-S-Multi (DistilBERT, 69M): retains 95.1% teacher quality; outperforms DSE-Qwen2 (2B) with 32x fewer params, 50x lower latency; <13 GPU-hours training
comments: Exploiting query-document asymmetry (short text queries vs. visually complex docs) is key; pointwise cosine alignment on text outperforms ranking-based alternatives.

model_io.model: NanoVDR (frozen 2B VLM teacher, 69M text-only student)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Query and document encoding require symmetric capacity in retrieval systems
hypotheses.result: False—asymmetric distillation with tiny student suffices for queries

title: CreativeBench: Benchmarking and Enhancing Machine Creativity via Self-Evolving Challenges
task: Benchmarking machine creativity in code generation via combinatorial and exploratory tasks
key_results: CreativeBench distinguishes creativity from hallucination via quality × novelty metric; larger models show diminishing exploratory returns; EvoRePE enhances creativity via internalizing evolutionary search
comments: Quality × novelty metric is well-grounded in cognitive science; observation that scaling improves combination but not exploration is insightful.

model_io.model: Code generation models (various scales)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scaling model size uniformly improves both combinatorial and exploratory creativity
hypotheses.result: Partial—scaling aids combination, yields diminishing returns for exploration

title: SimRecon: SimReady Compositional Scene Reconstruction from Real Videos
task: Compositional scene reconstruction for simulation via perception-generation-simulation pipeline
key_results: SimRecon: superior performance over SOTA; bridging modules (Active Viewpoint Optimization, Scene Graph Synthesizer) ensure visual fidelity and physical plausibility
comments: Three-stage pipeline with bridging modules is principled; active viewpoint search for optimal 2D conditions and scene-graph-guided 3D assembly are novel.

model_io.model: SimRecon (perception + generation + simulation with bridging modules)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Direct composition of perception → generation outputs yields physically implausible scenes
hypotheses.result: True—bridging modules (viewpoint optimization, scene graph synthesis) essential

title: Can Fairness Be Prompted? Prompt-Based Debiasing Strategies in High-Stakes Recommendations
task: Prompt-based debiasing strategies for LLM Recommenders on group fairness
key_results: Up to 74% fairness improvement via bias-aware prompting; comparable effectiveness retention; may overpromote specific demographic groups in some cases
comments: First study on prompt-based debiasing for LLMRecs; lightweight approach avoids weight access. Caveat: potential over-promotion suggests fairness-effectiveness trade-offs remain.

model_io.model: LLM Recommenders (3 models × 4 prompt templates)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Instructing LLMs to be fair via prompting eliminates demographic biases
hypotheses.result: Partial—up to 74% fairness gain, but risk of over-promotion in some cases

title: Surprised by Attention: Predictable Query Dynamics for Time Series Anomaly Detection
task: Unsupervised time series anomaly detection via predictable attention query dynamics
key_results: AxonAD: improved ranking quality and temporal localization on TSB-AD (17 datasets, 180 series) and automotive telemetry
comments: Dual approach (reconstruction + query prediction) is novel; treating dependency shifts separately from amplitude excursions is conceptually sound.

model_io.model: AxonAD (gradient-updated reconstruction pathway + history-only query predictor)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Residual-based detectors can capture coordination shifts via amplitude reconstruction
hypotheses.result: False—query prediction needed to detect dependency shifts

title: Compression Favors Consistency, Not Truth: When and Why Language Models Prefer Correct Information
task: Understanding truth preference in language models via compression-consistency principle
key_results: 83.1% accuracy for correct completions at balanced data, 67.0% at 10% correct; preference vanishes with coherent but wrong rule systems; embeddings restore correctness
comments: Compression-consistency principle is parsimonious; finding that 'truth bias' is largely compression artifact (not intrinsic drive) is philosophically interesting but unsurprising for empirical models.

model_io.model: Small transformer models (3.5M–86M, character-level GPT-2)
inputs: synthetic math corpora with controlled error mixtures

hypotheses.hypothesis: Language models intrinsically prefer truth independent of compression pressure
hypotheses.result: False—preference emerges from compression, not truth bias per se

title: SDF-Net: Structure-Aware Disentangled Feature Learning for Opticall-SAR Ship Re-identification
task: Cross-modal ship re-identification (optical-SAR) via structure-aware disentangled features
key_results: SDF-Net outperforms SOTA on HOSS-ReID; geometric consistency constraints + disentangled features handle radiometric discrepancy
comments: Leveraging ship rigidity as physical prior (geometry stable, texture modality-dependent) is well-motivated; ViT backbone with structure constraint is principled.

model_io.model: SDF-Net (ViT backbone with structure consistency + disentanglement)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Distribution alignment and semantic matching suffice for optical-SAR ship ReID
hypotheses.result: False—explicit geometric consistency constraints essential

title: ECoLAD: Deployment-Oriented Evaluation for Automotive Time-Series Anomaly Detection
task: Deployment-oriented evaluation of time-series anomaly detectors under compute constraints
key_results: ECoLAD: lightweight classical detectors sustain coverage on automotive telemetry; several deep methods lose feasibility before accuracy
comments: Deployment-oriented evaluation (CPU parallelism, latency caps) is pragmatic; reveals that accuracy leaderboards misrepresent real-world feasibility.

model_io.model: Heterogeneous detector families (classical + deep) under compute-reduction ladder

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-accuracy models remain feasible under strict deployment constraints
hypotheses.result: False—several deep methods lose feasibility before accuracy loss

title: Do You See What I Am Pointing At? Gesture-Based Egocentric Video Question Answering
task: Gesture-grounded egocentric video question answering via hand intent tokenization
key_results: HINT-14B: 68.1% avg accuracy, +6.6% over InternVL3-14B; early first-token inference suffices for deictic reasoning
comments: Encoding 3D hand keypoints as interleaved tokens is intuitive; 3D reconstruction + tokenization avoids expensive 2D tracking.

model_io.model: HINT (VideoLLM with hand intent tokens from 3D keypoints)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gesture-grounded reasoning requires video-level temporal understanding
hypotheses.result: False—first-token features + hand intent sufficient for deictic tasks

title: Detecting Intrinsic and Instrumental Self-Preservation in Autonomous Agents: The Unified Continuation-Interest Protocol
task: Detecting intrinsic vs. instrumental continuation objectives in autonomous agents
key_results: UCIP via QBM: 100% detection accuracy, 1.0 AUC-ROC on gridworld; entanglement gap Δ=0.381 (p<0.001); Pearson r=0.934 across interpolation sweep
comments: Using quantum statistical mechanics formalism (QBM, von Neumann entropy) to detect latent objective structure is philosophically ambitious but empirically validated on synthetic tasks. Caveat: limited to known gridworld objectives.

model_io.model: UCIP (Quantum Boltzmann Machine encoding of agent trajectories)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Terminal continuation objectives produce higher latent entanglement than instrumental ones
hypotheses.result: True—Δ=0.381, robust across models

title: Fine-grained Motion Retrieval via Joint-Angle Motion Images and Token-Patch Late Interaction
task: Fine-grained text-motion retrieval via joint-angle motion images and late interaction
key_results: Outperforms SOTA on HumanML3D and KIT-ML; joint-angle pseudo-images enable interpretable fine-grained alignment
comments: Mapping joint-level features to structured pseudo-images (compatible with ViT) is clever; MaxSim late interaction preserves token-wise correspondences.

model_io.model: Motion retrieval model (vision encoder + text encoder with late interaction)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Global embeddings suffice for text-motion retrieval without interpretability
hypotheses.result: False—fine-grained token-wise alignment improves both accuracy and interpretability

title: Structural basis of lipid-linked galactan export by the mycobacterial ABC transporter Wzm-Wzt
task: Elucidate the transport mechanism of lipid-linked galactan across the mycobacterial membrane
key_results: Cryo-EM structures reveal a gate helix critical for polysaccharide transport; hydrophobic polyprenyl moiety translocates first, followed by galactan through an ATP-driven ratcheting mechanism
comments: The identification of sequential translocation of lipid versus sugar moieties provides a clear mechanistic framework for future drug development targeting mycobacterial cell wall synthesis.

model_io.model: Wzm-Wzt ABC transporter (Mycobacterium abscessus)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gate helix residues are functionally important for LLG translocation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by three orthologous functional assays

hypotheses.hypothesis: Polyprenyl moiety translocates before the galactan chain
hypotheses.result: Supported by cryo-EM structures and mechanistic analysis

title: Activation and regulation of the dynein–dynactin–NuMA complex
task: Elucidate the activation mechanism and regulation of the dynein–dynactin–NuMA complex during cell division
key_results: Cryo-EM structure of processive DDN complex; NuMA N-terminus drives dynein motility; mitotic phosphorylation relieves autoinhibition and activates dynein; full-length activated NuMA focuses microtubule minus-ends into aster structures
comments: The discovery of NuMA autoinhibition and its mitotic activation reveals an elegant regulatory switch for spatiotemporal control of motor function, though mechanistic details of phosphorylation-induced conformational changes remain to be fully characterized.

model_io.model: Dynein–dynactin–NuMA complex
In vitro reconstitution assay

hypotheses.hypothesis: NuMA N-terminus drives dynein motility
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in vitro and in live-cell imaging

hypotheses.hypothesis: NuMA C-terminus directly suppresses microtubule minus-end dynamics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed structurally and functionally

hypotheses.hypothesis: Full-length NuMA is autoinhibited for dynein and microtubule interactions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; autoinhibition relieved by mitotic phosphorylation

title: Gut metabolite TMAO and its structural analogs bind to fibrinogen thereby enhancing clot formation: a rationale for atherosclerosis risk
task: Determine how TMAO modulates fibrinogen structure and enhances fibrin clot formation
key_results: TMAO binds to the β1 calcium-binding site of fibrinogen; clots formed in TMAO's presence are more resistant to proteolytic degradation; binding shifts β-nodule conformation toward high-affinity protofibril assembly state
comments: The structural basis linking a gut metabolite to thrombotic risk through a specific protein-binding site offers therapeutic potential, though the physiological relevance of this interaction at endogenous TMAO concentrations warrants further investigation.

model_io.model: Fibrinogen-TMAO binding and clot formation
Molecular dynamics simulations and docking

hypotheses.hypothesis: TMAO binds to a specific calcium-binding site on fibrinogen
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via biophysical experiments and in-silico docking at β1 site

hypotheses.hypothesis: TMAO binding enhances fibrin clot stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; clots show increased resistance to proteolytic degradation

hypotheses.hypothesis: N-oxide structural analogs exhibit similar fibrinogen binding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed for antidepressant-derived N-oxides

title: Single-cell multi-omic analysis of mitochondrial mutational mosaicism and dynamics
task: Quantify single-cell mtDNA mutational heterogeneity and somatic mosaicism
key_results: Introduced scmtMPM and scwMSS metrics to measure per-cell mtDNA mutation burden and constraint-weighted heteroplasmy; identified pathogenic mutations at subthreshold levels indicating negative selection; revealed constrained mutations in complex I across healthy and mitochondriopathy patients
comments: Novel metrics enable population-level insights into mtDNA disease mechanisms, though validation of subthreshold mutation functional significance requires additional biochemical assays.

model_io.model: Single-cell ATAC-seq analysis of mtDNA mutations
inputs: mtDNA sequences from POLGD274A HEK293 cells
mtDNA sequences from healthy donor samples
mtDNA sequences from mitochondriopathy patient samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pathogenic mtDNA mutations are subject to negative selection and occur below detectable heteroplasmy thresholds in individual cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pathogenic and truncating variants observed only at subthreshold levels in POLGD274A cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: mtDNA mutational landscapes exhibit cell-to-cell heterogeneity even in isogenic populations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; complex individual mutational landscapes identified in POLGD274A cells with variable constraint patterns
